Report.Comment: Division rear boundary – northern limit of 3SBCT AO
BCT left boundary – 1-68 AR left / 3-7 CAV right
BCT right boundary – 3-7 CAV left / 1-8 CAV right
Phase Line GOLD – LD/LC
Phase Line IRON – Passage of lines complete / BCT assumes lead
Phase Line SILVER – Trigger for direct support fires
Phase Line COPPER – BCT objective consolidation line
Checkpoint 1 – Siberia Road junction
Checkpoint 2 – Goldstone Road overpass
Checkpoint 3 – Bicycle Lake north gate
Checkpoint 4 – Valley Road wadi crossing
Checkpoint 5 – Outlet of Tiefort Basin
Objective IRON – OPFOR main defensive position, Bicycle Lake area. BCT main effort.
Objective GOLD – OPFOR reserve and logistics node, Tiefort Mountains eastern slope. Supporting effort 3-7 CAV.
Objective COBRA – OPFOR forward observation posts, southern Tiefort. 1-8 CAV supporting effort.
Axis of Advance IRON – 1-68 AR main effort, valley floor via Goldstone Road
Axis of Advance SABER – 3-7 CAV supporting effort, Tiefort eastern flank
Assembly Area WARHORSE – 1-68 AR attack position, north of Goldstone
Assembly Area SABER – 3-7 CAV attack position, Siberia area
Battle Position WARHORSE-1 – Alpha Company, 1-68 AR. Prepared position NE of Goldstone dry lake.
Battle Position WARHORSE-2 – Bravo Company, 1-68 AR. Prepared position, Goldstone western ridge.
Fire Support Coordination Line IRON – beyond this line fires require 3SBCT CDR coordination
Restrictive Fire Line ALPHA – 1-68 AR / 3-7 CAV boundary; fires across this line require adjacent CDR approval
No-Fire Area MAIN POST – Fort Irwin cantonment/garrison. No indirect fires within 5km of Main Post without CG approval.
Coordinated Fire Line IRON – forward limit of supporting fires during assault on OBJ IRON. Lifted on CDR order.
Airspace Coordination Area APACHE – 11th Attack Aviation corridor, active H+2 through H+6. Alt block 500-2000ft AGL. Route northbound only.
Main Supply Route GOLDEN – Fort Irwin Road / National Training Center primary LOC. Sustained 24/7 traffic, priority to CSS.
Alternate Supply Route IRON – Goldstone Road. Priority to 1-68 AR CSS and forward positioning.
Alternate Supply Route SABER – Siberia Road. Priority to 3-7 CAV CSS.
Protective minefield belt ALPHA – emplaced by 317th EN BN. Blocks NAI 4 approach. Marked per FM 3-34.210.
3-7 CAV screen line NEVADA – Provides early warning of OPFOR movement. Maintain 10km standoff from PL GOLD.
Named Area of Interest 1 – Goldstone Road approach. Trigger: OPFOR BN+ moving SE triggers FSCL adjustment.
Named Area of Interest 2 – Northern Bicycle Lake approach. Trigger: OPFOR arty observed displacing east of Tiefort.
Named Area of Interest 3 – Southern Tiefort pass. Trigger: OPFOR two or more vehicles identified in pass triggers OBJ COBRA branch.
Target Reference Point ANVIL – Prominent ridge NE of Bicycle Lake. Used by 1-7 FA for registration and shift of fires.
Target Reference Point HAMMER – Tiefort eastern nose. Used by 2-27 FA (MLRS) and 1-7 FA for quick fire missions.
Target Reference Point SWORD – Southern pass exit, Tiefort Mountains. 1-7 FA priority target for OBJ COBRA prep fires.
Passage Point NORTH – 1-68 AR forward passage of lines through 3-7 CAV screen. Anticipated H+1. IAW OPORD Annex C.
Passage Point SOUTH – 3-7 CAV route back through 1-68 AR AO after feint complete. Anticipated H+3.
Release Point IRON – 1-68 AR combat trains release point. CDR assumes control of CSS at this point.
Line of Contact WARHORSE – 1-68 AR / 3-7 CAV line of contact as of 071200ZMAY2026. Adjust as contact is made.
